title: Static backward-taken forward-not-taken branch prediction in IF stage
motivation:
The current core treats every branch as not-taken until EX resolution, paying
a full 2-cycle penalty on every taken branch. CoreMark's hot loops are
overwhelmingly backward branches (loop-closing BEQ/BNE with negative offset).
Adding a zero-state static predictor — predict taken when instr[31]==1 for
B-type opcodes — eliminates the 2-cycle bubble for ~85% of taken branches
with zero area overhead beyond a sign-bit check and an immediate adder in IF.
This is the classic textbook improvement from Patterson & Hennessy that
requires no PHT, no BTB, and no training logic — pure combinatorial addition.

hypothesis.motivation:
The current core treats every branch as not-taken until EX resolution, paying
a full 2-cycle penalty on every taken branch. CoreMark's hot loops are
overwhelmingly backward branches (loop-closing BEQ/BNE with negative offset).
Adding a zero-state static predictor — predict taken when instr[31]==1 for
B-type opcodes — eliminates the 2-cycle bubble for ~85% of taken branches
with zero area overhead beyond a sign-bit check and an immediate adder in IF.
This is the classic textbook improvement from Patterson & Hennessy that
requires no PHT, no BTB, and no training logic — pure combinatorial addition.
hypothesis.hypothesis:
Extend the IF stage to pre-decode the opcode and sign-bit of B-type branches.
When instr[6:0] == BRANCH and instr[31] == 1 (negative offset, i.e. backward
branch), redirect the PC immediately to PC + B_imm, bypassing the 2-cycle
wait for EX-stage branch resolution. The prediction flows through the pipeline
as a predicted_taken bit in if_id_t / id_ex_t. EX stage compares actual branch
direction against the prediction and fires a redirect only on mispredict
(redirecting to PC+4 on false-positive, or to branch_target on false-negative).
Correct predictions cost zero cycles; mispredicts pay the existing 2-cycle
penalty. No new state elements, no training — the PHT/BTBs are left to future
predictor-category hypotheses.

changes.description: Add logic predicted_taken field to if_id_t and id_ex_t packed structs so the branch prediction travels from IF through ID to EX for mispredict detection.

Add combinational branch detection (opcode == 7'b1100011) and static prediction (instr[31] == 1 for backward branches). Compute B-type immediate and bypass PC to predict_target on predicted-taken. Pass predicted_taken to if_id_t output.
Copy predicted_taken from the if_id_t input into the id_ex_t register so it reaches the EX stage for mispredict comparison.

Replace unconditional branch redirect with mispredict-based redirect: redirect fires only when branch_taken != predicted_taken (or on jumps). On false-positive mispredict redirect to pc+4. On false-negative mispredict redirect to branch_target.
Add logic predicted_taken field to if_id_t and id_ex_t packed structs so the branch prediction travels from IF through ID to EX for mispredict detection.

Add combinational branch detection (opcode == 7'b1100011) and static prediction (instr[31] == 1 for backward branches). Compute B-type immediate and bypass PC to predict_target on predicted-taken. Pass predicted_taken to if_id_t output.
Copy predicted_taken from the if_id_t input into the id_ex_t register so it reaches the EX stage for mispredict comparison.

Replace unconditional branch redirect with mispredict-based redirect: redirect fires only when branch_taken != predicted_taken (or on jumps). On false-positive mispredict redirect to pc+4. On false-negative mispredict redirect to branch_target.

lesson: - 2026-05-02 hyp-20260502-001-r1s0 (broken, n/a): Static branch prediction that redirects PC in IF stage breaks `pc_fwd` formal check — speculative PCs leak into RVFI traces; any control-flow speculation hypothesis must gate RVFI PC reporting on commit-time resolution, not fetch-time prediction.

title: Move branch resolution from EX to ID stage to halve taken-branch penalty
motivation:
The current 5-stage pipeline resolves all branches in the EX stage (stage 3),
incurring a full 2-cycle penalty per taken branch. CoreMark is branch-heavy
(~12% taken branches), wasting ~12-13% of issue slots on branch flushes.
Moving resolution one stage earlier (to ID) cuts this to 1 cycle, reclaiming
roughly half those lost slots with no prediction hardware.

hypothesis.motivation:
The current 5-stage pipeline resolves all branches in the EX stage (stage 3),
incurring a full 2-cycle penalty per taken branch. CoreMark is branch-heavy
(~12% taken branches), wasting ~12-13% of issue slots on branch flushes.
Moving resolution one stage earlier (to ID) cuts this to 1 cycle, reclaiming
roughly half those lost slots with no prediction hardware.
hypothesis.hypothesis:
By adding branch operand forwarding muxes and a comparator to the ID stage,
and adding a small new hazard (stall 1 cycle when branch depends on EX-stage
instruction), taken-branch penalty drops from 2 cycles to 1 cycle. The
comparator and forwarding logic removed from the EX stage partially offsets
the added ID-stage logic, keeping the critical path balance intact. Net IPC
gain for CoreMark should be ~5-8%.

changes.description:
Add branch operand forwarding muxes (2x 3:1, from regfile, EX/MEM, MEM/WB),
a 6-way branch comparator (BEQ/BNE/BLT/BGE/BLTU/BGEU), branch target adder,
and redirect output. Add a new RAW hazard input signal (branch_depends_on_ex)
that defers ID/EX capture when asserted, inserting a 1-cycle bubble so the
branch can forward from EX next cycle.

Remove branch resolution logic (comparator, branch_cond/taken/target
computation, branch redirect). Keep JAL/JALR resolution and ALU operand
forwarding intact.

Route fwd_ex_mem and fwd_mem_wb values to ID stage for branch forwarding.
Route ex_mem_rd/mem_wb_rd and their reg_write bits to the new ID-side
forwarding logic. Pipe the ID-stage redirect to both if_stage and
hazard_unit, OR-ing it with the remaining EX redirect for JAL/JALR.

Add new hazard: branch_in_id consumes rd produced by instruction currently
in EX stage. When detected, assert stall_if=1 and stall_id=1 so the branch
holds in ID for one cycle, then forwards from EX/MEM. Takes lower priority
than dmem_stall to preserve the existing stall precedence.

Add branch operand forwarding muxes (2x 3:1, from regfile, EX/MEM, MEM/WB),
a 6-way branch comparator (BEQ/BNE/BLT/BGE/BLTU/BGEU), branch target adder,
and redirect output. Add a new RAW hazard input signal (branch_depends_on_ex)
that defers ID/EX capture when asserted, inserting a 1-cycle bubble so the
branch can forward from EX next cycle.

Remove branch resolution logic (comparator, branch_cond/taken/target
computation, branch redirect). Keep JAL/JALR resolution and ALU operand
forwarding intact.

Route fwd_ex_mem and fwd_mem_wb values to ID stage for branch forwarding.
Route ex_mem_rd/mem_wb_rd and their reg_write bits to the new ID-side
forwarding logic. Pipe the ID-stage redirect to both if_stage and
hazard_unit, OR-ing it with the remaining EX redirect for JAL/JALR.

Add new hazard: branch_in_id consumes rd produced by instruction currently
in EX stage. When detected, assert stall_if=1 and stall_id=1 so the branch
holds in ID for one cycle, then forwards from EX/MEM. Takes lower priority
than dmem_stall to preserve the existing stall precedence.

lesson: - 2026-05-02 hyp-20260502-002-r1s1 (regression, +0.00%): Moving branch resolution from EX to ID exchanged a 2-cycle taken-branch flush for a 1-cycle data-dependent stall; CoreMark's branch operands are nearly always produced by the immediately preceding instruction, so every taken branch triggers the stall and nets zero IPC gain.

title: Gshare branch predictor with BTB
motivation:
The current core has no branch predictor — it always predicts not-taken
and pays a 2-cycle misprediction penalty on every taken branch (and JAL/JALR).
CoreMark has ~15-20% conditional branches with ~50-60% taken rate, plus
frequent JAL calls/returns. A gshare predictor can achieve ~92-95% accuracy
on CoreMark, eliminating most of the ~0.15-0.20 CPI penalty. This is the
single highest-ROI microarchitectural feature missing from the baseline.

hypothesis.motivation:
The current core has no branch predictor — it always predicts not-taken
and pays a 2-cycle misprediction penalty on every taken branch (and JAL/JALR).
CoreMark has ~15-20% conditional branches with ~50-60% taken rate, plus
frequent JAL calls/returns. A gshare predictor can achieve ~92-95% accuracy
on CoreMark, eliminating most of the ~0.15-0.20 CPI penalty. This is the
single highest-ROI microarchitectural feature missing from the baseline.
hypothesis.hypothesis:
Add a gshare branch predictor: an N-bit global history register (GHR) XORed
with PC bits indexes a pattern history table (PHT) of 2-bit saturating
counters. A small direct-mapped BTB caches target addresses for recently
taken branches. Prediction occurs in IF stage: if BTB hit and PHT says taken,
redirect fetch to BTB target. JAL is always predicted taken with immediate
target. On branch resolution in EX, update GHR, PHT, and BTB; on mispredict,
flush the pipeline and restore correct PC. Keep area minimal: 64-256 PHT
entries, 8-16 BTB entries, 6-8 bit GHR. This should improve IPC by 5-12%
with negligible Fmax impact.

changes.description: New module: gshare predictor with GHR, PHT of 2-bit saturating counters, and direct-mapped BTB. Provides predict_taken and predict_target outputs, accepts update signals from EX stage.
Integrate predictor output into fetch PC selection. On predicted taken branch, redirect PC to predict_target instead of pc+4. Maintain speculative state for recovery on mispredict.
On branch resolution, produce predictor update signals: actual branch direction, actual target, and PC for index computation. Signal mispredict to trigger pipeline flush.
Wire branch_predictor into the pipeline: connect predictor to IF stage, connect EX stage update signals back to predictor, route mispredict flush through hazard unit.
Add predictor configuration parameters (GHR width, PHT depth, BTB depth) and any needed struct definitions for predictor interfaces.
New module: gshare predictor with GHR, PHT of 2-bit saturating counters, and direct-mapped BTB. Provides predict_taken and predict_target outputs, accepts update signals from EX stage.
Integrate predictor output into fetch PC selection. On predicted taken branch, redirect PC to predict_target instead of pc+4. Maintain speculative state for recovery on mispredict.
On branch resolution, produce predictor update signals: actual branch direction, actual target, and PC for index computation. Signal mispredict to trigger pipeline flush.
Wire branch_predictor into the pipeline: connect predictor to IF stage, connect EX stage update signals back to predictor, route mispredict flush through hazard unit.
Add predictor configuration parameters (GHR width, PHT depth, BTB depth) and any needed struct definitions for predictor interfaces.
